title: SandboxAdapterRegistry + selection policy + SandboxSelectionFailure event
goal: Build the central registry that holds every available SandboxAdapter instance and the selection policy that picks an adapter for a ProcessSpec per spec §3.5.3 (operator default = WSL2PodmanAdapter; per-job override via Work Profile knob OR ProcessSpec.required_capabilities; WindowsNativeJailAdapter when win32_native_fidelity required; DockerAdapter only on explicit operator opt-in; silent fallback is FORBIDDEN; failure emits typed SandboxSelectionFailure event).
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/registry.rs (SandboxAdapterRegistry struct) + src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/selection.rs (selection policy + SandboxSelectionFailure event) + tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/sandbox_selection_tests.rs. mod.rs re-exports.
unblock_conditions: Shared types + SandboxAdapter trait merged; registry can hold trait objects.
implementation_notes: SandboxAdapterRegistry: `pub struct SandboxAdapterRegistry { adapters: BTreeMap<AdapterId, Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>>, default_adapter_id: AdapterId, docker_explicit_opt_in: bool }`. Constructor: `SandboxAdapterRegistry::new(default_adapter_id: AdapterId) -> Self`. Mutation surface: `register(adapter: Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>)` (panics on duplicate AdapterId so registration mistakes fail loud), `set_docker_explicit_opt_in(opt_in: bool)`. Read surface: `list() -> Vec<AdapterCapabilities>`, `get(adapter_id: &AdapterId) -> Option<Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>>`, `default() -> Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>`. Selection policy lives in selection.rs: `pub fn select(registry: &SandboxAdapterRegistry, spec: &ProcessSpec, work_profile_override: Option<&AdapterId>) -> Result<Arc<dyn SandboxAdapter>, SandboxSelectionFailure>`. Algorithm: (1) if work_profile_override is Some, attempt that adapter and verify its capabilities satisfy spec.required_capabilities; if no match, emit SandboxSelectionFailure::OverrideCapabilityMismatch; (2) else if spec.required_capabilities contains win32_native_fidelity, attempt windows_native_jail; (3) else if spec.required_capabilities contains DockerOnly (a sentinel RequiredCapability variant added in this MT only if not already present from MT-041 — coordinate via MT-041 errata if needed), require docker_explicit_opt_in==true and use docker; (4) else use registry.default(). For every candidate, run `candidate.capabilities()` and check each `spec.required_capabilities` entry is satisfied (mapping rules: win32_native_fidelity -> caps.win32_native_fidelity==true; nvidia_cuda_passthrough -> caps.gpu_passthrough==NvidiaCuda; vendor_agnostic_gpu -> caps.gpu_passthrough!=None; cross_machine_portable -> caps.cross_machine_portable==true; very_strong_filesystem_isolation -> caps.filesystem_isolation_strength==VeryStrong; very_strong_network_isolation -> caps.network_isolation_strength==VeryStrong; high_stdio_throughput -> caps.stdio_throughput_class==High). If any required capability fails, emit SandboxSelectionFailure::CapabilityUnsatisfied{ required, available_by_adapter }. NO silent fallback to default; failure is loud per spec §3.5.3. SandboxSelectionFailure enum (thiserror Error + Serialize): CapabilityUnsatisfied{ required: Vec<RequiredCapability>, available_by_adapter: BTreeMap<AdapterId, AdapterCapabilities> }, AdapterNotRegistered{ adapter_id: AdapterId }, DockerNotExplicitlyOptedIn, OverrideCapabilityMismatch{ override_id: AdapterId, required: Vec<RequiredCapability> }. Selection failures MUST emit an EventLedger row of family `FR-EVT-SANDBOX-SELECT-FAIL` for HBR-INT-001 replay (consumer of the event is wired later in MT-050/MT-053). Tests: (a) default adapter is returned when no overrides and empty required_capabilities, (b) win32_native_fidelity routes to windows_native_jail, (c) Docker selection fails when docker_explicit_opt_in==false, (d) capability mismatch emits CapabilityUnsatisfied (NOT silent fallback), (e) work_profile_override forces a specific adapter or fails loud.
risk_if_missed: Without selection policy, callers reach for the first adapter they can `get()` and the spec §3.5.3 'silent fallback is forbidden' rule is violated; Docker becomes accidental default.
validator_focus: Silent-fallback test: a ProcessSpec demanding win32_native_fidelity on a registry containing only WSL2Podman MUST return SandboxSelectionFailure, not WSL2Podman; Docker opt-in test: a ProcessSpec preferring docker MUST fail when docker_explicit_opt_in==false.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: No silent fallback test: capability mismatch returns SandboxSelectionFailure and does NOT silently return the default adapter.
Docker opt-in test asserts a packet that did not call set_docker_explicit_opt_in(true) cannot reach the DockerAdapter.
Registry duplicate-register call panics (registration mistakes fail loud at startup, not silently).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Registry + selection policy fail-loud on capability mismatch, refuse Docker without explicit opt-in, panic on duplicate registration, and emit a structured SandboxSelectionFailure event family for replay; no silent fallback.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Registry + selection policy fail-loud on capability mismatch, refuse Docker without explicit opt-in, panic on duplicate registration, and emit a structured SandboxSelectionFailure event family for replay; no silent fallback.
